prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `aee291af`.
codex_response:
Implemented `aee291af`. The rule is: scan for square windows with an all-`8` border and an interior made only of `8/2` with at least one `2`, then return the least-common such window as the odd one out. I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint and discarded the `arc2_sonnet45_summary.jsonl` overlay hint because the official examples extract a unique full square rather than unioning partial frames; see [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_aee291af/notes.md).

No new primitive was needed. The verifier is in [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_aee291af/verifier.py), and the generator in [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_aee291af/generator.py) explicitly places 3-4 same-sized bordered squares, repeats one interior pattern, inserts one distinct pattern, adds attached/free `8` noise, and rejects any sample that creates unintended extra valid windows. Wiring is in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_aee291af/__init__.py).

Verification passed: `4/4` official examples and `1000/1000` generated examples. Outputs are in [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/aee291af/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/aee291af/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/aee291af/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/aee291af/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/aee291af/previews/preview_03.png).
